name: poly_bevel_G3_partial_fan
description: Polygon Bevel (mesh.bevel, edit-mode Polygons) GROUP shared-corner accumulator, PARTIAL branch (internalCnt>=2 && anyBoundary) -- task 0458 Phase 1. Valence-4 apex fan (4 irregular quads around P), only 3 of the 4 quads selected, so at P the selection has 2 internal edges (shared with 2 neighbouring selected faces) AND 2 boundary edges (shared with the 4th, unselected quad) -- a cube cannot produce this shape (a cube corner is always fully enclosed once 3 mutually-adjacent faces are selected), so this is a raw_mesh. group=true, inset=0.1, shift=0.1. Reference topology: 9v/4f -> 17v/12f -- THE central finding this fixture pins down: the reference SHARES P into ONE new vertex (referenced by all 5 incident new faces: the 3 replaced selected faces + the 2 boundary bridge quads), where vibe3d's pre-0458 kernel fell through this branch entirely and split P into 3 SEPARATE per-face vertices instead (a TOPOLOGY divergence, not just a numeric one -- the pre-fix vertex count was 19, not 17). This fixture asserts the corrected topology (vertex/face counts, which uniquely distinguish 'shared' from 'split') via expected_before/expected_after. It intentionally does NOT assert per-vertex positions: P's exact position needs a further 'boundaryInset' displacement term beyond orig+shift*AVE_N whose closed form was not pinned to float precision even with rr/gdb read access to the reference (toolcards/poly.bevel/findings.md SS1 -- measured ~0.128 on this exact case, not a derivable formula) -- a documented, known gap, not silently papered over with a loosened tolerance. (The kernel change here is topology-only: it now shares one vertex at orig+shift*AVE_N, the same closed form as poly_bevel_G2_apex_v3's fully-enclosed apex, without the missing boundary term.)
source: Frozen reference-editor capture via the private capture harness's polygon-bevel op (task 0458 Phase 0 dump-oracle poly_bevel_G3_partial_fan).

provenance.harness: private capture harness (reference-editor polygon-bevel tool, headless scripted op-dump)
provenance.notes: Phase-0 dump-oracle for the group shift accumulator's partial branch -- the pre-0458 kernel had NO code path here at all (fell through to the per-face default), producing a topology divergence (3 split vertices vs the reference's 1 shared vertex). This fixture pins the corrected topology (17v/12f) via vertex/face counts; positions are a documented, known gap (see source/mesh.d's bevelFacesByMask doc comment) not asserted here.
